description:
**Problem**
Operators need introspection into store health, watermarks, segments, replication, and checkpoints.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md Â§16.1 (admin.status, admin.metrics)

**Design**
- admin.status returns: store_id, replica_id, namespaces, applied/durable watermarks, WAL segment stats (count/bytes), index health (last_indexed_offset per segment), replication sessions (peers, last_ack, lag), checkpoint groups (dirty/in-flight/last push)
- admin.metrics returns: counters (wal_append_ok/err, apply_ok/err, repl_events_in/out), gauges (IPC inflight, repl queue depth, per-peer lag), histograms (wal_append_duration, checkpoint_duration)
- Both return structured JSON

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.status returns complete store introspection
- [ ] admin.metrics returns counters/gauges/histograms
- [ ] CLI `bd admin status` and `bd admin metrics` work
- [ ] Tests verify expected fields

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/daemon/admin.rs (new), src/cli/commands/admin.rs (new)
title: Phase 7: admin.status + admin.metrics IPC ops

description:
**Problem**
Graph/metadata command families are still split across legacy `beads-rs` handlers (`comments`, `dep`, `label`, `epic`, `deleted`, `subscribe`), which blocks a clean ownership boundary for CLI behavior.

**Design**
Port these command handlers into `crates/beads-cli/src/commands/` in one tranche focused on behavior parity and shared render/helper reuse. Leave explicit shims in `beads-rs` until the full epic closes.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `comments`, `dep`, `label`, `epic`, `deleted`, and `subscribe` handlers are owned by `beads-cli`.
- [ ] Legacy handlers in `beads-rs` are reduced to call-through shims.
- [ ] Subcommand name mapping and aliases remain stable.
- [ ] Regression tests cover dep tree/cycles, comment add/list, label add/rm/list, epic status, and deleted/subscription flows.
- [ ] Full verification gate passes (`fmt`, `dylint`, `clippy`, `test`, `slow-tests`).

**Files**
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/comments.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/dep.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/label.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/epic.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/deleted.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/subscribe.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/cli/commands/{comments,dep,label,epic,deleted,subscribe}.rs
title: Migrate comments/dep/label/epic/deleted/subscribe handlers to beads-cli

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Previous checkpoint payload is validated before reuse in export path.
- [ ] Invalid previous payload falls back to full export automatically.
- [ ] Invalid checkpoint payload is not persisted in cache/memory as reusable state.
- [ ] Tests cover invalid-previous fallback and no repeated publish-fail loop.
description:
**Problem**
Checkpoint export/publish can reuse previous checkpoint payload before strict validation, which can create repeated publish failures instead of self-healing full rebuild.

**Files**: crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/git_worker.rs, crates/beads-git/src/checkpoint/publish.rs
design:
**Design**
Harden previous-payload reuse:
- validate `previous` payload with strict import before reuse
- if invalid, drop `previous` and export full (`previous: None`)
- avoid caching in-memory invalid export payloads before successful publish

Ensure publish path converges to a healthy checkpoint without manual intervention.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ f0de8dc3: publish_checkpoint now validates any reusable previous export before reuse, invalidates bad in-memory or cached previous state, falls back to full export automatically, and does not persist new reusable state until checkpoint ref publish succeeds. Added daemon coverage for invalid cached previous, invalid in-memory previous, and failed-publish non-persistence. Workspace gates are green (`cargo test`, `just dylint`, `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`).

title: Migration slice 7: Harden checkpoint previous-payload reuse

description:
**Problem**
IPC request handling has no per-request span, so errors and downstream logs (handle_request, read gates, durability waits) lack request context (repo, request type, namespace, actor, client_request_id).

**Design**
- Add a small RequestContext extractor in `src/daemon/server.rs` that derives request_type + key fields.
- Create a `tracing::info_span!("ipc_request", ...)` in `process_request_message` and execute `daemon.handle_request` + follow-on handling inside it.
- For durability waiters, carry the span so the eventual response/logs are in the same span.
- Keep fields lowâ€‘cardinality (request_type, repo, namespace, actor_id, client_request_id, read_consistency).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Request handling logs include an `ipc_request` span with the fields above.
- [ ] Durability wait responses/logs are emitted within the same span.
- [ ] Unit test (or focused helper test) verifies RequestContext extraction for at least Create + Show.

**Files:** src/daemon/server.rs, src/daemon/ipc/types.rs (if helper added), src/daemon/executor.rs (if needed)
title: Observability: per-IPC request span with request metadata

description: Forward sequence gaps can advance max/next while contiguous watermark lags. Replace implicit gap tolerance with explicit gap error state; disallow committing next-origin-seq from non-contiguous state.
title: P0: Forbid non-contiguous replay progression from committing allocator next-seq

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Internal repl message types cannot be constructed without valid head/seq combinations.
- [ ] Decode rejects `seq>0` without head and `seq=0` with head (clear error).
- [ ] Encoding preserves canonical ordering and wire compatibility.
- [ ] Tests cover valid/invalid ACKs and HELLO/WELCOME watermark snapshots.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.
description:
**Problem**
Replication wire structs (`Ack`, `Hello`, `Welcome`) carry `WatermarkMap` plus optional `WatermarkHeads`. There is no type-level coupling between `seq` and `head`, so it is easy to construct invalid states (e.g., `seq>0` with no head). The compiler cannot prevent this; errors only surface later as runtime failures (`MissingHead` / divergence), and these failures are not localized to the parsing boundary.

This violates parse-donâ€™t-validate and makes correctness depend on call-site discipline.

**Impact**
- Incorrect or missing heads can stall replication or mark peers diverged.
- Internal code can accidentally emit invalid ACKs without compile-time feedback.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/proto.rs` (Ack/Hello/Welcome decode)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs` (watermark snapshot/seed)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs`
design:
**Design**
Introduce a typed, invariant-preserving watermark representation at the Repl boundary:

- Create an internal type that represents *validated* watermarks (use `Watermarks<K>` or a new `WatermarkSnapshot<K>` that contains `Watermark<K>` values).
- Keep wire format unchanged by introducing wire-only structs:
  - `WireAck` / `WireHello` / `WireWelcome` with `WatermarkMap` + `WatermarkHeads` (serde/cbor only)
  - `Ack` / `Hello` / `Welcome` used internally with `Watermarks<K>` (or validated snapshot type)
- `decode_*` should parse into wire structs, then immediately validate/convert into the typed representation:
  - Missing head for seq>0 becomes a `ProtoDecodeError::InvalidField`.
  - Genesis (`seq=0`) must have no head (or Genesis marker), enforced by `Watermark::new`.
- `encode_*` should accept the typed representation and derive wire maps/heads in canonical order.

**Ordering / invariants preserved**
- Maintain deterministic ordering by using `BTreeMap` during wire conversion.
- No change to wire encoding order or shape; only validation is moved to the parse boundary.
title: Repl watermark heads must be coupled to seq at parse boundary

description:
**Problem**
Even with the general F0.3 envelope drop, \`bd ready --json\` has a second layer of nesting. Current output:

\`\`\`json
{\"result\":\"ready\",\"data\":{\"issues\":[...],\"blocked_count\":N,\"closed_count\":N}}
\`\`\`

Go emits a bare array: \`[...]\`. Gascity's Ready() (bdstore.go:735-750) calls \`parseIssuesTolerant(extractJSON(out))\` which expects the top-level to be an array of issues. The nested \`.data.issues\` path is inaccessible.

Reference: docs/go-parity/cmds/gascity/11-ready.md.

**Design**
\`bd ready --json\` emits a bare array of IssueSummary objects. The \`blocked_count\` + \`closed_count\` sidecar stats go one of three places:

Option A â€” available via separate commands: \`bd count --blocked\`, \`bd count --status=closed\`.

Option B â€” available via a flag: \`bd ready --json --with-stats\` restores the current nested shape.

Option C â€” drop entirely; beads-rs gains nothing gascity-parity by emitting them in Go's shape since Go doesn't.

Recommendation: A + C (drop from ready output, expose via count if consumers want them). Keep the IPC-level rich shape for library consumers if useful; only the CLI wire is simplified.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`bd ready --json | jq -e 'type'\` returns \`\"array\"\`
- [ ] \`bd ready --json | jq -e '.[0].id'\` works
- [ ] If stats are preserved behind a flag, \`bd ready --json --with-stats | jq -e '.blocked_count'\` works
- [ ] No consumer of the current \`ReadyResult\` shape inside Rust breaks (or is updated deliberately)
- [ ] \`cargo xtest\` green

**Independent of**
F0.3 (general envelope drop). This is the per-command shape fix on top of that.

**Files likely touched**
- \`crates/beads-cli/src/commands/ready.rs\`
- \`crates/beads-api/src/issues.rs\` (\`ReadyResult\` â€” may drop or keep for IPC)
title: Floor 0 wire: bd ready emit bare array (drop {issues,blocked_count,closed_count} nesting)

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] REPL e2e fixtures no longer derive temp roots from long repo paths.
- [ ] `cargo test --features slow-tests` no longer fails with `path must be shorter than SUN_LEN` in long workspaces.
- [ ] Keep-tmp debugging still works.
closed_reason: REPL integration temp roots are now short, shared, and guarded against Unix socket path overflow.
description:
**Problem**
`cargo test --features slow-tests` currently fails in the REPL e2e integration suite when the repo lives under a long path, because `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs` hardcodes its temp root to `current_dir()/tmp`. On macOS this produces Unix socket paths longer than `SUN_LEN`, so `bd init` fails with `IO error: path must be shorter than SUN_LEN` before the tests exercise replication behavior.

This reproduces reliably in long `jj` workspace paths such as `/Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover`.

**Files**: crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs
design:
**Design**
Make the REPL e2e harness choose a short temp root that is independent of the repo path.

1. Replace `current_dir()/tmp` with a short system temp root or an explicit env override.
2. Keep the ability to retain temp dirs for debugging (`BD_TEST_KEEP_TMP`).
3. Add a regression test or harness assertion proving socket/runtime paths stay under `SUN_LEN` even from long workspace paths.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
The REPL slow harness no longer derives temp roots from the workspace path.

Landed on the current stack:
- shared `fixtures::temp` picks a short per-user `/tmp/...` root by default, with `BD_TEST_TMP_ROOT` as the explicit override and `BD_TEST_KEEP_TMP=1` preserved for debugging
- `repl_rig`, `daemon/logging`, and the tailnet trace harness now share that temp-root policy instead of open-coding `current_dir()/tmp`
- `repl_rig` asserts that daemon socket paths stay within the Unix socket path limit before booting nodes
- `AGENTS.md` now documents the shared temp-root rule so new tests stay on the same fixture path

Fresh verification from `/Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs`:
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration fixtures::temp::tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration daemon::logging --features slow-tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e::repl_tailnet_proxy_smoke -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e::repl_daemon_to_daemon_roundtrip -- --nocapture`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow -p beads-rs --test integration --all-features --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e:: --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `13.013s`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `50.389s`

title: REPL slow-test tmp root should not overflow SUN_LEN in long workspaces
